### Post by FoolFromHell on 2007-05-07
I tried everything.
Im new to Ubuntu, but I think I got my drivers set up.
Everything is running perfectly except my resolution wont be allowed to go past 1024x768 when my monitor supports 1280x1024 and I can do the full 1280x1024 on Windows.
Here is my xorg.conf

Can somebody help me?

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.

	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"

EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"

	Driver		"mouse"

	Identifier	"stylus"

	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"

	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"nVidia Corporation G80 [GeForce 8800 GTX]"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"nVidia Corporation G80 [GeForce 8800 GTX]"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"

	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4

	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"

	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

### Post by FoolFromHell on 2007-05-07
Oh wait, nevermind.
I changed the driver from nvidia to vesa and it works at 1280x1024 now.
Is there much of a difference between the nvidia driver and vesa?

---

### Post by jerrylamos on 2007-05-07
There sure is.  Vesa can be very slow in some systems.  Try scrolling.  I don't have your graphics controller to see what drivers would work. You can do a lspci or lspci -v to check what Ubuntu thinks the controller is, and then possibly look at the manufacturer's site for a driver.  I do recommend:

Ctrl-Alt-F1
sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
will give you menus where you can select driver and screen resolution.  If it seems to work,
sudo killall gdm
sudo startx

instead of directly editing xorg.conf

Good luck, Jerry
Cheers, Jerry

---

### Post by scott_g on 2007-05-07
If you want to use the proprietary nVidia drivers, you can likely use a command line tool called gtf.  

Its usage is:

gtf resolution refresh

eg:
gtf 1280 1024 60

then paste the result under the monitor section in your xorg.conf file.

Worked for me at least...

Scott

---
